Bélgica y España protagonizan un choque de gigantes por las semifinales
items.summary: La previa del Bélgica vs España, uno de los partidos más atractivos de los cuartos de final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. Además, repasamos el antecedente entre ambas selecciones y las declaraciones de Gavi y Borja Iglesias antes del encuentro.

items.title: Kylian Mbappé vs. Marruecos: cámara exclusiva con sus mejores movimientos
items.summary: El capitán francés registro un partido de claroscuros, luego de fallar un penal, marcó un golazo y asistió en otro, aquí te traemos todas sus acciones.

items.title: Deschamps: “Tengo jugadores fantásticos; de lo contrario, no habríamos llegado hasta aquí”
items.summary: Con un tercer pase consecutivo a semifinales, Didier Deschamps resalta el trabajo de sus jugadores. Sobre Mbappé y Koné, explica que sus salidas del campo se deben a la suma de los partidos a lo largo del torneo.

items.title: Man charged with killing National Guard member hospitalized after refusing food
items.summary: Federal prosecutors say a man accused of shooting two National Guard troops near the White House, killing one of them, has been taken to a hospital for treatment of a potentially life-threatening medical condition after he refused to eat food in jail

items.title: Mbappé y Dembélé brillan con golazos en la victoria de Francia sobre Marruecos.
items.summary: Los cracks de Francia aparecieron en el momento clave. Kylian Mbappé y Ousmane Dembélé firmaron dos auténticos golazos para sellar el 2-0 sobre Marruecos en los Cuartos de Final.

items.title: Francia frena a Marruecos y se instala entre los cuatro mejores | Todo el Mundial
items.summary: Miguel Gurwitz y Andrés Guardado analizaron la victoria de Francia sobre Marruecos, resultado que los clasificó a las semifinales de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. Además, debatieron el desempeño del conjunto marroquí.

items.title: Kylian Mbappé: “Estoy aquí para ganar”
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé habló tras el triunfo de Francia ante Marruecos y aseguró que la misión todavía no termina. El capitán francés reconoció la dificultad de ver eliminado a Achraf Hakimi, pero dejó claro que en el Mundial no hay espacio para relajarse.

items.title: Conexión Mundial | Francia impone su jerarquía y ya está en semifinales
items.summary: Los expertos analizaron el 2-0 de Francia sobre Marruecos, debatieron el penal sobre Mbappé y repasaron la lucha del francés por la Bota de Oro y los récords mundialistas.

items.title: Mohamed Ouahbi aseguró que Francia fue mejor que Marruecos y la eliminación es justa
items.summary: Marruecos fue una de las mejores selecciones durante la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026, pero no pudo mantener el ritmo en cuartos de final cuando enfrentaron a Francia e incluso su director técnico no pudo hacer otra cosa más que reconocer al rival.

items.title: ¡Icónico! Jurgen Klopp felicita a Kylian Mbappé por el triunfo y comparten un gran momento en Boston
items.summary: El ex entrenador del Liverpool, Jurgen Klopp comparte un memorable momento al compartir palabras de aliento y risas con Kylian Mbappé, quien fue la figura para el triunfo francés.

items.title: France downs Morocco 2-0 to advance to the World Cup semifinal
items.summary: Morocco was no match for France, which lost 2-0. The French, one of the pre-tournament favorites, move on to the World Cup semifinals against either Spain or Belgium.

items.title: Man’s hand blown is off as he cleans July Fourth debris off California beach
items.summary: A man lost his hand when an explosive device detonated while he was cleaning up Fourth of July firework debris on a Northern California beach.

items.title: Mbappé abre el marcador y Dembélé lo sigue con otro tanto
items.summary: En menos de 10 minutos, los goleadores franceses han anotado goles que acercan a ‘Les Bleus’ a semis y a una posible tercera final consecutiva. El entrenador Deschamps celebra y los jugadores abrazan a Dembélé durante el segundo gol.

items.title: We tested dozens of Brooks’ popular sneakers — here’s what we really think (and what can improve)

items.title: Smithsonian chief emphasizes 'accuracy and integrity' after White House report
items.summary: The memo from the Smithsonian's secretary, Lonnie Bunch, responded to a White House report that calls the National Museum of American History driven by "a radical, activist ideology."

items.title: Prosecutors show video from interview with Tyler Robinson's former roommate
items.summary: In a pretrial hearing for Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk, prosecutors shared video from a recorded interview authorities had conducted with Lance Twiggs, Robinson's former roommate and romantic partner.

items.title: How to deal with seesawing gas prices
items.summary: Gas prices have fluctuated since the U.S. and Israel launched a war on Iran, which disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and left consumers unsure of what they'll pay at the pump.

items.title: EPA proposes weakening heavy-duty truck pollution rules
items.summary: The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.

items.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit challenging Tuberville's eligibility to run for Alabama governor
items.summary: A judge has dismissed a lawsuit alleging U.S. Sen. Tommy Tuberville does not meet the residency requirement to run for governor of Alabama

items.title: Flight instructor jumped midair to his death, left student to land the plane
items.summary: Argentine authorities are investigating the suicide of Leandro Andrés Bertazzo, who reportedly hadn’t told his boss he’d been under psychiatric care.

items.title: A new kind of robot swims the seas and soars the skies
items.summary: Inspired by diving birds, roboticists built the lightweight machines to move from water to air. The design may one day lead to robots that can monitor and sample the coastal ocean.

items.title: Vermont is creating a statewide trail, and making sure it's accessible to all
items.summary: Mountain bike enthusiasts have been working for years on an ambitious 485-mile, multi-use trail called The Velomont that will span the length of the state. They're making sure it's friendly for people with disabilities, particularly cyclists.

items.title: Charlie Kirk assassination suspect expressed regret after shooting, roommate says
items.summary: The suspect, Tyler Robinson, ultimately turned himself in to law enforcement. He has been charged with aggravated murder.

items.title: Class action suit against AI makers over deepfake child sexual abuse material expands

items.title: Darrell Sheets, ‘Storage Wars’ star who died by suicide, left note about cyberbully
items.summary: Police said in May that Sheets died by suicide. At the time, officials said they were looking into “allegations of cyberbullying.”

items.title: 'Solo agers' are a growing group. Changes that would help them could help everyone
items.summary: Many older adults navigate aging on their own — without children or a spouse. An expert says this is "a reality to be supported" rather than a crisis to be solved.

items.title: Former Olympian pleads not guilty in reflecting pool vandalism charge

items.summary: The United States and Iran exchanged a new round of intensive back-and-forth strikes hours after President Donald Trump declared he thinks the ceasefire is now over. NBC’s Raf Sanchez reports for TODAY.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.
